title: typhales
intro: The taxonomical rank of Typhales is an order of monocotyledonous plants, characterized by the presence of a single cotyledon and typically having parallel veins in their leaves. This order includes various aquatic and terrestrial plants with distinctive growth habits, such as the Typha genus. Some examples of families within the Typhales order are the Typhaceae, which includes the cattails, and the Cyperaceae, which includes the sedges. The order also includes the Canna and the Spathiphyllum, although the Canna is not part of the Typhaceae family, and the Spathiphyllum is not part of the Cyperaceae family, but the Spathiphyllum is not part of the Typhaceae family, and the Canna is not part of the Cyperaceae family, but the orchidaceae and euphorbiaceae are not part of the Typhales order.

families.family_desc: Typhaceae is a family of aquatic plants commonly known as cattails, consisting of species typically found in wetlands and shallow water.
